(Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. - Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

name: joon-robotics-competition-project
description: What the /home/user/joon project is — robotics competition perception on Jetson Orin Nano
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: c1b83ff7-86ff-4467-adfb-c2717d5ef135

`/home/user/joon` is a perception pipeline for a **robotics competition**: a robot in a 4×4 m arena autonomously finds, identifies, picks up, and places target objects, all computed onboard an **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano**.

- **Two separate Sets** (do NOT merge into one model): **Set 1** = white 3D-printed polyhedra (cube/octahedron/dodecahedron/icosahedron); **Set 2** = cubes with fruit textures. Only one target shape is announced on competition day; load only that set's models.
- **Set 1 architecture (built)**: two-stage = high-recall YOLO11n detector (single class `polyhedron`) → conservative MobileNetV3-Small shape classifier (5 classes incl. `unknown`) → IoU tracker → conservative decision policy (`SEARCHING`/`TARGET_CONFIRMED`/`PICKUP_READY`/`GIVE_UP`). Never pick on an ambiguous shape; dodecahedron-vs-icosahedron is the key confusion.
- **Sensors**: 2D LiDAR @0.30 m (nav only, not shape), two **NUROUM V11** RGB cameras (1280×720, 90° HFOV, fx=fy=640) @~0.20 m, −20° pitch, forward-outward ±30°. Synthetic data must be from these robot viewpoints.
- **Environment**: Isaac Sim 5.1 runs on **Windows** (not WSL); training/runtime in the WSL `yolo/` venv (ultralytics 8.4.78, torch 2.11 CUDA, RTX 4070 Ti Super). User: snurobotteam2@gmail.com.
- Source STLs: 6C1=cube, 8C1=octahedron, 12C1_Fixed=dodecahedron, 20C1=icosahedron.

Set 1 is implemented end-to-end (see `README_SET1.md`). Set 2 is a future separate pipeline reusing shared `sim/ deployment/ runtime/`. See [[isaac-sim-51-gotchas]].

---
name: set2-fruit-cube-pipeline
description: "Set 2 perception build — fruit-cube detector+classifier, key design decisions"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: a960d078-1290-4dc8-b6c6-73de9b4a5d38
---

Set 2 (fruit cubes) two-stage pipeline is implemented, mirroring Set 1 and kept fully
separate (own configs/models/datasets/runtime). See [[joon-robotics-competition-project]].

Key design decisions (non-obvious, worth keeping):
- Cube fruit faces are rendered as **thin textured decal quads** flush on 3 of 6 faces of a
  procedural white box (not STL+GeomSubsets — STL has no UVs). White-face-only `unknown`
  views then fall out of camera geometry naturally.
- The `fruit` vs `unknown` crop label is decided **analytically** in `sim/fruit_cube.py`
  (`CameraModel` + `fruit_visibility`): which fruit faces point at the camera and their
  projected area ratio — NOT from the renderer's bbox of the fruit. Model learns visible-fruit
  recognition, never hidden-cube guessing.
- Cubes are driven each frame via direct USD xform ops (like Set 1's camera); only the optional
  non-cube negatives use the Set-1-proven `rep.new_layer()` + `on_frame` trigger path.
- Decision policy is false-positive averse (wrong pickup −40): never picks `unknown`, REJECTs on
  ≥2 strong other-fruit votes, requests viewpoint change after 4 consecutive unknowns.

Open items: confirm real cube edge (`cubes.size_m`, default 0.06) and whether fruit-face layout
is fixed (`cubes.fixed_face_layout`); replace placeholder fruit textures in
`assets/fruit_textures/<fruit>/` with real photos. The Isaac generator is untestable in WSL/yolo
venv (needs Isaac python); decision policy + texture gen were verified locally.

11	
12	- **`rep.create.light` produces NO illumination** in this build → renders come out pure black. Fix: create lights with the USD API directly (`UsdLux.DistantLight`/`DomeLight` on the stage) and randomize their intensity via `.GetIntensityAttr().Set(...)`.
13	- **RGB capture is black unless the renderer accumulates** → drive frames with `rep.orchestrator.step(rt_subframes=32)` in a Python loop, NOT `rep.orchestrator.run()` + `simulation_app.update()` pumping (that gives 1 sample → black).
14	- **`rgb` annotator may return float [0,1]** → must `*255 .astype(uint8)` before saving or the PNG is black. (Also add a mean-brightness gate to drop occasional glitch frames.)
15	- `asset_converter.create_converter_context()` was removed → use `AssetConverterContext()`.
16	- `trigger.on_frame(num_frames=...)` warns it's deprecated for `max_execs` (still works).
17	- **Windows CMD (`python.bat`) can't use a `\\wsl.localhost\...` UNC cwd** → copy the repo to `C:\joon` first, generate there, robocopy results back to WSL.
18	- STL→USD: normalize to a known size with op order `[Translate(-center), Scale]` (scale-about-center); the reverse order mis-places the mesh. Our 4 solids normalize to 0.2 m max-extent.
19	
20	See [[joon-robotics-competition-project]].

file.content:
---
name: isaac-sim-51-gotchas
description: "Hard-won fixes for Isaac Sim 5.1 Replicator synthetic-data generation (black frames, lights, run loop)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: reference
  originSessionId: c1b83ff7-86ff-4467-adfb-c2717d5ef135
---

Isaac Sim **5.1** (Omniverse standalone at `C:\Users\user\Documents\IsaacSim\python.bat`) Replicator gotchas, discovered while building the polyhedra data generator. All encoded in `isaac/generate_replicator.py` and `sim/generate_set1_data.py`:

- **`rep.create.light` produces NO illumination** in this build → renders come out pure black. Fix: create lights with the USD API directly (`UsdLux.DistantLight`/`DomeLight` on the stage) and randomize their intensity via `.GetIntensityAttr().Set(...)`.
- **RGB capture is black unless the renderer accumulates** → drive frames with `rep.orchestrator.step(rt_subframes=32)` in a Python loop, NOT `rep.orchestrator.run()` + `simulation_app.update()` pumping (that gives 1 sample → black).
- **`rgb` annotator may return float [0,1]** → must `*255 .astype(uint8)` before saving or the PNG is black. (Also add a mean-brightness gate to drop occasional glitch frames.)
- `asset_converter.create_converter_context()` was removed → use `AssetConverterContext()`.
- `trigger.on_frame(num_frames=...)` warns it's deprecated for `max_execs` (still works).
- **Windows CMD (`python.bat`) can't use a `\\wsl.localhost\...` UNC cwd** → copy the repo to `C:\joon` first, generate there, robocopy results back to WSL.
- STL→USD: normalize to a known size with op order `[Translate(-center), Scale]` (scale-about-center); the reverse order mis-places the mesh. Our 4 solids normalize to 0.2 m max-extent.

See [[joon-robotics-competition-project]].

"""Onboard perception entry point. Loads ONLY the selected set's models.

    python deployment/run_perception.py --set set1 --target dodecahedron --source 0
    python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --source 0
    python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --left 0 --right 1

For Set 1: detect white polyhedra from afar, classify shape only when close/reliable,
vote across frames, and announce TARGET_CONFIRMED / PICKUP_READY conservatively.

For Set 2: detect cube candidates from afar, classify the visible fruit only when close
and the fruit is actually visible, never pick an 'unknown' cube, request a viewpoint
change when a cube stays unknown, and confirm the target fruit across frames before pickup.
"""

import argparse
import os
import sys

import yaml

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
sys.path.insert(0, ROOT)

STATE_COLOR = {"SEARCHING": (180, 180, 180), "TARGET_CONFIRMED": (0, 200, 255),
               "PICKUP_READY": (0, 230, 0), "GIVE_UP": (0, 0, 200),
               # Set 2 states:
               "UNKNOWN_CUBE": (160, 160, 160), "NON_TARGET_FRUIT": (0, 120, 220),
               "TARGET_CANDIDATE": (0, 200, 255), "REJECTED": (0, 0, 200)}


def run_set1(cfg, args):
    import cv2
    from runtime.set1_pipeline import Set1Pipeline
    from runtime.logging import FailureLogger

    if args.target not in cfg["classes"]["shapes"]:
        raise SystemExit(f"--target must be one of {cfg['classes']['shapes']}")
    pipe = Set1Pipeline(cfg, args.target)
    logger = FailureLogger(os.path.join(ROOT, "runtime_logs", "set1"), enabled=args.log)
    print(f"[set1] target={args.target}; loaded detector + classifier. Press q to quit.")

    src = int(args.source) if str(args.source).isdigit() else args.source
    cap = cv2.VideoCapture(src)
    while cap.isOpened():
        ok, frame = cap.read()
        if not ok:
            break
        for r in pipe.process_frame(frame):
            x0, y0, x1, y1 = (int(v) for v in r["bbox"])
            color = STATE_COLOR.get(r["state"], (200, 200, 200))
            label = f"{r['cls'] or 'polyhedron'} {r['conf']:.2f} {r['state']}"
            cv2.rectangle(frame, (x0, y0), (x1, y1), color, 2)
            cv2.putText(frame, label, (x0, max(0, y0 - 6)),
                        cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.5, color, 1)
            if r["state"] == "PICKUP_READY":
                print(f"PICKUP_READY: {args.target} @ bbox {r['bbox']}  info={r['info']}")
            if args.log and (r["cls"] == "unknown" or (r["cls"] and r["margin"] < cfg["runtime"]["margin_threshold"])):
                logger.maybe_log(cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB), r, reason=r["cls"] or "lowmargin")
        if args.show:
            cv2.imshow("set1", frame)
            if cv2.waitKey(1) & 0xFF == ord("q"):
                break
    cap.release()
    cv2.destroyAllWindows()


def run_set2(cfg, args):
    """Detect cube candidates, classify visible fruit conservatively, confirm target.

    Reads from one or two cameras (--left/--right, or --source for a single camera).
    A cube 'unknown' in one camera can be identified by the other; a cube that stays
    unknown raises a re-observe request the navigator should honour by moving."""
    import cv2
    from runtime.set2_pipeline import Set2Pipeline
    from runtime.logging import FailureLogger

    fruits = cfg["classes"]["fruits"]
    if args.target not in fruits:
        raise SystemExit(f"--target must be one of {fruits}")
    pipe = Set2Pipeline(cfg, args.target)
    logger = FailureLogger(os.path.join(ROOT, "runtime_logs", "set2"), enabled=args.log)

    # Camera sources: prefer explicit --left/--right; else a single --source as 'left'.
    cams = {}
    if args.left is not None:
        cams["left"] = cv2.VideoCapture(int(args.left) if str(args.left).isdigit() else args.left)
    if args.right is not None:
        cams["right"] = cv2.VideoCapture(int(args.right) if str(args.right).isdigit() else args.right)
    if not cams:
        src = int(args.source) if str(args.source).isdigit() else args.source
        cams["left"] = cv2.VideoCapture(src)
    print(f"[set2] target={args.target}; cameras={list(cams)}; loaded detector + classifier.")

    while all(c.isOpened() for c in cams.values()):
        any_ok = False
        for cam_name, cap in cams.items():
            ok, frame = cap.read()
            if not ok:
                continue
            any_ok = True
            for r in pipe.process_frame(frame, camera=cam_name):
                x0, y0, x1, y1 = (int(v) for v in r["bbox"])
                color = STATE_COLOR.get(r["state"], (200, 200, 200))
                label = f"{cam_name[:1]} {r['cls'] or 'cube'} {r['conf']:.2f} {r['state']}"
                cv2.rectangle(frame, (x0, y0), (x1, y1), color, 2)
                cv2.putText(frame, label, (x0, max(0, y0 - 6)),
                            cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.5, color, 1)
                if r["state"] == "PICKUP_READY":
                    print(f"PICKUP_READY: {args.target} @ {cam_name} bbox {r['bbox']} info={r['info']}")
                if r["request_reobserve"]:
                    print(f"RE-OBSERVE: track {r['track']} on {cam_name} stays unknown -> "
                          f"move to a new viewpoint. info={r['info']}")
                    # In a full system the navigator moves, then calls:
                    #   pipe.note_reobserved(cam_name, r['track'])
                if args.log and (r["cls"] == "unknown"
                                 or (r["cls"] and r["margin"] < cfg["runtime"]["margin_threshold"])):
                    logger.maybe_log(cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB), r,
                                     reason=r["cls"] or "lowmargin")
            if args.show:
                cv2.imshow(f"set2-{cam_name}", frame)
        if args.show and (cv2.waitKey(1) & 0xFF == ord("q")):
            break
        if not any_ok:
            break
    for c in cams.values():
        c.release()
    cv2.destroyAllWindows()


def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--set", dest="set_name", required=True, choices=["set1", "set2"])
    ap.add_argument("--config", default=None)
    ap.add_argument("--target", default="dodecahedron",
                    help="announced target: set1 shape or set2 fruit")
    ap.add_argument("--source", default="0", help="camera index or video/image path")
    ap.add_argument("--left", default=None, help="set2: left camera index/path")
    ap.add_argument("--right", default=None, help="set2: right camera index/path")
    ap.add_argument("--show", action="store_true")
    ap.add_argument("--log", action="store_true", help="save uncertain crops for the data loop")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    cfg_path = args.config or os.path.join(ROOT, "configs", f"{args.set_name}.yaml")
    cfg = yaml.safe_load(open(cfg_path, encoding="utf-8"))

    if args.set_name == "set1":
        run_set1(cfg, args)
    else:
        run_set2(cfg, args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

2	# Set 1 perception config - white 3D-printed polyhedra in a 4x4 m arena.
3	# Every tunable for the Isaac Sim data generation lives here so the sim files
4	# stay generic. Edit values to match the real robot / competition arena.
5	# ============================================================================
6	
7	set: set1
8	
9	classes:
10	  shapes: [cube, octahedron, dodecahedron, icosahedron]   # classifier shape classes
11	  detector: [polyhedron]                                   # detector: single class
12	  unknown: unknown                                         # classifier reject class
13	
14	# Geometry assets (already normalized to 0.2 m by isaac/convert_stl_to_usd.py).
15	assets:
16	  usd_dir: isaac/assets/usd
17	  usd_by_class:
18	    cube: cube.usd
19	    octahedron: octahedron.usd
20	    dodecahedron: dodecahedron.usd
21	    icosahedron: icosahedron.usd
22	
23	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- arena
24	arena:
25	  size_x: 4.0           # m
26	  size_y: 4.0           # m
27	  wall_height: 0.5      # m
28	  wall_thickness: 0.05  # m
29	  floor_color: [0.45, 0.30, 0.17]   # wood-ish brown (UsdPreviewSurface diffuse)
30	  floor_color_jitter: 0.08
31	  floor_roughness: [0.5, 0.9]
32	  wall_color: [0.92, 0.92, 0.92]    # white walls
33	  wall_color_jitter: 0.06
34	  wall_roughness: [0.4, 0.8]
35	  spawn_margin: 0.35    # keep objects/robot this far from walls (m)
36	
37	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- camera (NUROUM V11)
38	camera:
39	  width: 1280
40	  height: 720
41	  hfov_deg: 90.0
42	  focal_length: 10.4775       # mm  (HFOV 90 with horizontal_aperture below)
43	  horizontal_aperture: 20.955 # mm
44	  # Intrinsics (for reference / metadata): fx=fy=640, cx=640, cy=360
45	  fx: 640.0
46	  fy: 640.0
47	  cx: 640.0
48	  cy: 360.0
49	
50	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- robot sensor rig
51	# Camera mounts are defined relative to base_link. Two side cameras aimed
52	# forward-outward. Per-frame mount jitter simulates real mounting error.
53	robot:
54	  base_height: 0.0            # base_link z on the floor
55	  cam_height: 0.20            # m  (RGB cameras)
56	  cam_forward_offset: 0.10    # m  (+x, ahead of base_link)
57	  cam_lateral_offset: 0.15    # m  (|y| to each side)
58	  cam_pitch_deg: -20.0        # downward
59	  cam_yaw_outward_deg: 30.0   # left=+30, right=-30 (forward-outward)
60	  lidar_height: 0.30          # m  (metadata only; not used for shape)
61	  occlusion_proxy: false      # robot-body occluders (disabled for now)
62	  # Mount-error randomization (added per frame):
63	  jitter:
64	    height: [-0.01, 0.01]     # m
65	    pitch_deg: [-7.0, 7.0]    # -> effective pitch -13..-27 deg
66	    yaw_deg: [-3.0, 3.0]
67	    roll_deg: [-2.0, 2.0]
68	
69	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- objects / placement
70	objects:
71	  max_per_class: 1            # pool = max_per_class * 4 shapes
72	  # Real printed sizes (longest side, metres) from the STL extents. Each shape is rendered
73	  # at its TRUE size so apparent sizes match the real robot view (octahedron is ~1.7x a cube).
74	  real_size_m:
75	    cube: 0.080
76	    octahedron: 0.136
77	    dodecahedron: 0.098
78	    icosahedron: 0.098
79	  render_calib_m: 64.9        # empirical: rep scale 1.0 renders the (0.2-unit) USD at ~64.9 m
80	  scale_range: [0.85, 1.15]   # then multiplied by this (per-frame size variation)
81	  # Placement is anywhere on the arena floor within spawn_margin of the walls.
82	  z_on_floor: 0.0
83	  # White 3D-printed plastic material (UsdPreviewSurface).
84	  material:
85	    base_color: [0.88, 0.88, 0.88]
86	    color_jitter: 0.06        # slight off-white variation per frame
87	    roughness: [0.35, 0.7]
88	    metallic: 0.0
89	
90	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- lighting / domain randomization
91	lighting:
92	  sun_intensity: [2000, 9000]
93	  sun_pitch_deg: [-70, -20]
94	  sun_yaw_deg: [-180, 180]
95	  dome_intensity: [600, 2500]
96	  color_temp: [4000, 8000]    # K
97	
98	postprocess:
99	  # Applied as label-preserving augmentation at TRAIN time (not in sim) unless noted.
100	  motion_blur: true
101	  gaussian_noise: true
102	  exposure_jitter: true
103	
104	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- dataset output
105	dataset:
106	  root: datasets/set1
107	  detector_subdir: detector       # images/{train,val} + labels/{train,val}
108	  classifier_subdir: classifier   # {train,val}/<class>/*.png
109	  metadata_subdir: metadata       # per-frame json
110	  val_ratio: 0.15
111	  min_frame_brightness: 12        # drop render-glitch (near-black) frames
112	
113	# Rules that turn a detected object into a classifier sample vs `unknown`.
114	labeling:
115	  # A crop becomes its true shape label only if ALL hold; else -> unknown.
116	  min_box_px: 32                  # min(w,h) in pixels for a valid shape crop
117	  min_visible_frac: 0.55          # 1 - occlusionRatio
118	  max_truncation_px: 2            # box edge this close to image border = truncated
119	  # Detector keeps boxes down to this size (high recall, incl. tiny/distant):
120	  detector_min_box_px: 8
121	  # Classifier crop augmentation (simulate detector imperfection):
122	  crop_margin_frac: [-0.08, 0.20] # negative=tight, positive=loose
123	  crop_shift_frac: 0.10           # random center shift
124	  crops_per_object: 2
125	  # Fraction of frames that also emit a background 'unknown' crop:
126	  background_unknown_frac: 0.3
127	
128	render:
129	  rt_subframes: 32
130	
131	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- runtime (Jetson)
132	runtime:
133	  detector_conf: 0.25           # low -> high recall; classifier rejects junk
134	  detector_imgsz: 640
135	  # Only classify a crop when the object is close/large enough to be reliable:
136	  min_bbox_px: 40               # min(w,h) in pixels
137	  min_bbox_area_ratio: 0.004    # bbox area / image area
138	  reject_truncation_px: 3       # skip detections this close to image border
139	  classifier_imgsz: 128
140	  # Conservative shape acceptance:
141	  conf_threshold: 0.60          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1
142	  margin_threshold: 0.20        # top1 - top2 probability
143	  # Tracking + voting:
144	  track_iou: 0.3
145	  track_max_age: 10             # frames a track survives unseen
146	  vote_window: 7                # recent classifications considered
147	  min_confirmations: 3          # target votes needed for TARGET_CONFIRMED
148	  max_reobserve: 6              # approach/re-observe attempts before giving up
149	  # PICKUP_READY gating:
150	  pickup_min_bbox_px: 90        # object must be close (large) to pick
151	  reconfirm_within: 3           # must be re-seen as target within N recent frames

# Set 1 perception config - white 3D-printed polyhedra in a 4x4 m arena.
# Every tunable for the Isaac Sim data generation lives here so the sim files
# stay generic. Edit values to match the real robot / competition arena.
# ============================================================================

set: set1

classes:
  shapes: [cube, octahedron, dodecahedron, icosahedron]   # classifier shape classes
  detector: [polyhedron]                                   # detector: single class
  unknown: unknown                                         # classifier reject class

# Geometry assets (already normalized to 0.2 m by isaac/convert_stl_to_usd.py).
assets:
  usd_dir: isaac/assets/usd
  usd_by_class:
    cube: cube.usd
    octahedron: octahedron.usd
    dodecahedron: dodecahedron.usd
    icosahedron: icosahedron.usd

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- arena
arena:
  size_x: 4.0           # m
  size_y: 4.0           # m
  wall_height: 0.5      # m
  wall_thickness: 0.05  # m
  floor_color: [0.45, 0.30, 0.17]   # wood-ish brown (UsdPreviewSurface diffuse)
  floor_color_jitter: 0.08
  floor_roughness: [0.5, 0.9]
  wall_color: [0.92, 0.92, 0.92]    # white walls
  wall_color_jitter: 0.06
  wall_roughness: [0.4, 0.8]
  spawn_margin: 0.35    # keep objects/robot this far from walls (m)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- camera (NUROUM V11)
camera:
  width: 1280
  height: 720
  hfov_deg: 90.0
  focal_length: 10.4775       # mm  (HFOV 90 with horizontal_aperture below)
  horizontal_aperture: 20.955 # mm
  # Intrinsics (for reference / metadata): fx=fy=640, cx=640, cy=360
  fx: 640.0
  fy: 640.0
  cx: 640.0
  cy: 360.0

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- robot sensor rig
# Camera mounts are defined relative to base_link. Two side cameras aimed
# forward-outward. Per-frame mount jitter simulates real mounting error.
robot:
  base_height: 0.0            # base_link z on the floor
  cam_height: 0.20            # m  (RGB cameras)
  cam_forward_offset: 0.10    # m  (+x, ahead of base_link)
  cam_lateral_offset: 0.15    # m  (|y| to each side)
  cam_pitch_deg: -20.0        # downward
  cam_yaw_outward_deg: 30.0   # left=+30, right=-30 (forward-outward)
  lidar_height: 0.30          # m  (metadata only; not used for shape)
  occlusion_proxy: false      # robot-body occluders (disabled for now)
  # Mount-error randomization (added per frame):
  jitter:
    height: [-0.01, 0.01]     # m
    pitch_deg: [-7.0, 7.0]    # -> effective pitch -13..-27 deg
    yaw_deg: [-3.0, 3.0]
    roll_deg: [-2.0, 2.0]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- objects / placement
objects:
  max_per_class: 1            # pool = max_per_class * 4 shapes
  # Real printed sizes (longest side, metres) from the STL extents. Each shape is rendered
  # at its TRUE size so apparent sizes match the real robot view (octahedron is ~1.7x a cube).
  real_size_m:
    cube: 0.080
    octahedron: 0.136
    dodecahedron: 0.098
    icosahedron: 0.098
  render_calib_m: 64.9        # empirical: rep scale 1.0 renders the (0.2-unit) USD at ~64.9 m
  scale_range: [0.85, 1.15]   # then multiplied by this (per-frame size variation)
  # Placement is anywhere on the arena floor within spawn_margin of the walls.
  z_on_floor: 0.0
  # White 3D-printed plastic material (UsdPreviewSurface).
  material:
    base_color: [0.88, 0.88, 0.88]
    color_jitter: 0.06        # slight off-white variation per frame
    roughness: [0.35, 0.7]
    metallic: 0.0

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- lighting / domain randomization
lighting:
  sun_intensity: [2000, 9000]
  sun_pitch_deg: [-70, -20]
  sun_yaw_deg: [-180, 180]
  dome_intensity: [600, 2500]
  color_temp: [4000, 8000]    # K

postprocess:
  # Applied as label-preserving augmentation at TRAIN time (not in sim) unless noted.
  motion_blur: true
  gaussian_noise: true
  exposure_jitter: true

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- dataset output
dataset:
  root: datasets/set1
  detector_subdir: detector       # images/{train,val} + labels/{train,val}
  classifier_subdir: classifier   # {train,val}/<class>/*.png
  metadata_subdir: metadata       # per-frame json
  val_ratio: 0.15
  min_frame_brightness: 12        # drop render-glitch (near-black) frames

# Rules that turn a detected object into a classifier sample vs `unknown`.
labeling:
  # A crop becomes its true shape label only if ALL hold; else -> unknown.
  min_box_px: 32                  # min(w,h) in pixels for a valid shape crop
  min_visible_frac: 0.55          # 1 - occlusionRatio
  max_truncation_px: 2            # box edge this close to image border = truncated
  # Detector keeps boxes down to this size (high recall, incl. tiny/distant):
  detector_min_box_px: 8
  # Classifier crop augmentation (simulate detector imperfection):
  crop_margin_frac: [-0.08, 0.20] # negative=tight, positive=loose
  crop_shift_frac: 0.10           # random center shift
  crops_per_object: 2
  # Fraction of frames that also emit a background 'unknown' crop:
  background_unknown_frac: 0.3

render:
  rt_subframes: 32

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- runtime (Jetson)
runtime:
  detector_conf: 0.25           # low -> high recall; classifier rejects junk
  detector_imgsz: 640
  # Only classify a crop when the object is close/large enough to be reliable:
  min_bbox_px: 40               # min(w,h) in pixels
  min_bbox_area_ratio: 0.004    # bbox area / image area
  reject_truncation_px: 3       # skip detections this close to image border
  classifier_imgsz: 128
  # Conservative shape acceptance:
  conf_threshold: 0.60          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1
  margin_threshold: 0.20        # top1 - top2 probability
  # Tracking + voting:
  track_iou: 0.3
  track_max_age: 10             # frames a track survives unseen
  vote_window: 7                # recent classifications considered
  min_confirmations: 3          # target votes needed for TARGET_CONFIRMED
  max_reobserve: 6              # approach/re-observe attempts before giving up
  # PICKUP_READY gating:
  pickup_min_bbox_px: 90        # object must be close (large) to pick
  reconfirm_within: 3           # must be re-seen as target within N recent frames

"""Set 1 runtime perception: detector -> crop -> conservative classifier -> track -> vote.

Loads only the Set 1 models (set1_polyhedron_detector + set1_shape_classifier). The
detector (ultralytics, .pt/.onnx/.engine) finds polyhedra at high recall; each crop is
classified by MobileNetV3 (torch .pt or .onnx) with temperature-calibrated confidence;
results feed the tracker + DecisionPolicy.
"""

import json
import os

import numpy as np

from runtime.tracking import Tracker
from runtime.decision_policy import DecisionPolicy

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))


def build_classifier_torch(num_classes):
    import torch.nn as nn
    from torchvision import models
    m = models.mobilenet_v3_small(weights=None)
    m.classifier[3] = nn.Linear(m.classifier[3].in_features, num_classes)
    return m


class ShapeClassifier:
    """Loads a MobileNetV3 classifier (.pt or .onnx) + calibration metadata."""

    def __init__(self, model_dir):
        self.classes = json.load(open(os.path.join(model_dir, "classes.json")))
        meta = json.load(open(os.path.join(model_dir, "temperature.json")))
        self.T = meta["temperature"]
        self.imgsz = meta["imgsz"]
        self.mean = np.array(meta["mean"], np.float32)
        self.std = np.array(meta["std"], np.float32)

        pt, onnx = os.path.join(model_dir, "best.pt"), os.path.join(model_dir, "best.onnx")
        if os.path.isfile(onnx):
            import onnxruntime as ort
            self.backend = "onnx"
            self.sess = ort.InferenceSession(onnx, providers=[
                "TensorrtExecutionProvider", "CUDAExecutionProvider", "CPUExecutionProvider"])
            self.inp = self.sess.get_inputs()[0].name
        else:
            import torch
            self.backend = "torch"
            self.torch = torch
            self.device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
            self.model = build_classifier_torch(len(self.classes)).to(self.device).eval()
            self.model.load_state_dict(torch.load(pt, map_location=self.device))

    def _pre(self, crop_rgb):
        import cv2
        img = cv2.resize(crop_rgb, (self.imgsz, self.imgsz)).astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        img = (img - self.mean) / self.std
        return np.transpose(img, (2, 0, 1))[None].astype(np.float32)

    def predict(self, crop_rgb):
        x = self._pre(crop_rgb)
        if self.backend == "onnx":
            logits = self.sess.run(None, {self.inp: x})[0][0]
        else:
            with self.torch.no_grad():
                logits = self.model(self.torch.from_numpy(x).to(self.device)).cpu().numpy()[0]
        z = logits / self.T
        e = np.exp(z - z.max()); p = e / e.sum()
        order = p.argsort()[::-1]
        top1, top2 = order[0], order[1]
        return self.classes[top1], float(p[top1]), float(p[top1] - p[top2])


class Set1Pipeline:
    def __init__(self, cfg, target_shape):
        from ultralytics import YOLO
        rt = cfg["runtime"]
        self.rt = rt
        self.target = target_shape
        self.detector = YOLO(os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set1", "detector", "best.pt"))
        self.clf = ShapeClassifier(os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set1", "classifier"))
        self.tracker = Tracker(rt["track_iou"], rt["track_max_age"], rt["vote_window"])
        self.policy = DecisionPolicy(rt, target_shape)
        self.frame_idx = 0

    def _classifiable(self, box, W, H):
        x0, y0, x1, y1 = box
        if min(x1 - x0, y1 - y0) < self.rt["min_bbox_px"]:
            return False
        if (x1 - x0) * (y1 - y0) / (W * H) < self.rt["min_bbox_area_ratio"]:
            return False
        m = self.rt["reject_truncation_px"]
        if x0 <= m or y0 <= m or x1 >= W - m or y1 >= H - m:
            return False
        return True

    def process_frame(self, frame_bgr):
        """Run one frame. Returns list of {bbox, state, cls, conf, info}."""
        import cv2
        H, W = frame_bgr.shape[:2]
        rgb = cv2.cvtColor(frame_bgr, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
        res = self.detector.predict(frame_bgr, conf=self.rt["detector_conf"],
                                    imgsz=self.rt["detector_imgsz"], verbose=False)[0]
        boxes = [tuple(map(float, b)) for b in res.boxes.xyxy.cpu().numpy()] if res.boxes else []

        matched = self.tracker.update(boxes, self.frame_idx)
        out = []
        for track, box in matched:
            cls, conf, margin = None, 0.0, 0.0
            if self._classifiable(box, W, H):
                x0, y0, x1, y1 = (int(max(0, box[0])), int(max(0, box[1])),
                                  int(min(W, box[2])), int(min(H, box[3])))
                crop = rgb[y0:y1, x0:x1]
                if crop.size:
                    cls, conf, margin = self.clf.predict(crop)
                    track.add_obs(cls, conf, margin, self.frame_idx)
            state, info = self.policy.evaluate(track)
            out.append({"bbox": box, "state": state, "cls": cls,
                        "conf": conf, "margin": margin, "info": info})
        self.frame_idx += 1
        return out

14	is left to the navigation layer; pass world_pos to process_frame to enable proximity

"""Set 2 runtime perception: detector -> crop -> conservative fruit classifier
-> per-camera track -> vote -> Set2DecisionPolicy.

Loads ONLY the Set 2 models (set2_cube_detector + set2_fruit_classifier). The
detector (ultralytics .pt/.onnx/.engine) finds cube candidates at high recall; each
crop is classified by MobileNetV3 (torch .pt or .onnx) into apple/orange/banana/
pineapple/unknown with temperature-calibrated confidence; results feed an IoU tracker
and the conservative decision policy.

Dual cameras: call process_frame(frame, camera='left'|'right'). A separate tracker per
camera keeps IoU association valid (the two views don't share image coordinates); a
cube that is 'unknown' in one camera can be classified by the other. Cross-camera
fusion of the SAME physical cube needs a world-position estimate (LiDAR/odometry) and
is left to the navigation layer; pass world_pos to process_frame to enable proximity
fusion if you have it.
"""

import json
import os

import numpy as np

from runtime.tracking import Tracker
from runtime.set2_decision_policy import Set2DecisionPolicy

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))


def build_classifier_torch(num_classes):
    import torch.nn as nn
    from torchvision import models
    m = models.mobilenet_v3_small(weights=None)
    m.classifier[3] = nn.Linear(m.classifier[3].in_features, num_classes)
    return m


class FruitClassifier:
    """Loads a MobileNetV3 fruit classifier (.pt or .onnx) + calibration metadata."""

    def __init__(self, model_dir):
        self.classes = json.load(open(os.path.join(model_dir, "classes.json")))
        meta = json.load(open(os.path.join(model_dir, "temperature.json")))
        self.T = meta["temperature"]
        self.imgsz = meta["imgsz"]
        self.mean = np.array(meta["mean"], np.float32)
        self.std = np.array(meta["std"], np.float32)

        pt, onnx = os.path.join(model_dir, "best.pt"), os.path.join(model_dir, "best.onnx")
        if os.path.isfile(onnx):
            import onnxruntime as ort
            self.backend = "onnx"
            self.sess = ort.InferenceSession(onnx, providers=[
                "TensorrtExecutionProvider", "CUDAExecutionProvider", "CPUExecutionProvider"])
            self.inp = self.sess.get_inputs()[0].name
        else:
            import torch
            self.backend = "torch"
            self.torch = torch
            self.device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
            self.model = build_classifier_torch(len(self.classes)).to(self.device).eval()
            self.model.load_state_dict(torch.load(pt, map_location=self.device))

    def _pre(self, crop_rgb):
        import cv2
        img = cv2.resize(crop_rgb, (self.imgsz, self.imgsz)).astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        img = (img - self.mean) / self.std
        return np.transpose(img, (2, 0, 1))[None].astype(np.float32)

    def predict(self, crop_rgb):
        x = self._pre(crop_rgb)
        if self.backend == "onnx":
            logits = self.sess.run(None, {self.inp: x})[0][0]
        else:
            with self.torch.no_grad():
                logits = self.model(self.torch.from_numpy(x).to(self.device)).cpu().numpy()[0]
        z = logits / self.T
        e = np.exp(z - z.max()); p = e / e.sum()
        order = p.argsort()[::-1]
        top1, top2 = order[0], order[1]
        return self.classes[top1], float(p[top1]), float(p[top1] - p[top2])


class Set2Pipeline:
    def __init__(self, cfg, target_fruit):
        from ultralytics import YOLO
        rt = cfg["runtime"]
        self.rt = rt
        self.target = target_fruit
        # Prefer the TensorRT engine, then ONNX, then the PyTorch checkpoint.
        det_dir = os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set2", "detector")
        det = next((os.path.join(det_dir, f"best.{e}") for e in ("engine", "onnx", "pt")
                    if os.path.isfile(os.path.join(det_dir, f"best.{e}"))), None)
        if det is None:
            raise SystemExit(f"no detector weights in {det_dir} (best.engine/onnx/pt)")
        print(f"[set2] detector backend: {os.path.basename(det)}")
        self.detector = YOLO(det)
        self.clf = FruitClassifier(os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set2", "classifier"))
        self.trackers = {}                         # camera -> Tracker (per-view association)
        self.policy = Set2DecisionPolicy(rt, target_fruit)
        self.frame_idx = 0

    def _tracker(self, camera):
        if camera not in self.trackers:
            self.trackers[camera] = Tracker(self.rt["track_iou"], self.rt["track_max_age"],
                                            self.rt["vote_window"])
        return self.trackers[camera]

    def _classifiable(self, box, W, H):
        """Only classify a cube that is close/large/untruncated enough to read fruit."""
        x0, y0, x1, y1 = box
        if min(x1 - x0, y1 - y0) < self.rt["min_bbox_px"]:
            return False
        if (x1 - x0) * (y1 - y0) / (W * H) < self.rt["min_bbox_area_ratio"]:
            return False
        m = self.rt["reject_truncation_px"]
        if x0 <= m or y0 <= m or x1 >= W - m or y1 >= H - m:
            return False
        return True

    def note_reobserved(self, camera, track_id):
        """Navigator calls this after it has moved the robot to honour a re-observe request."""
        tracker = self.trackers.get(camera)
        if tracker is None:
            return
        for t in tracker.tracks:
            if t.id == track_id:
                t.reobserve_count = getattr(t, "reobserve_count", 0) + 1

    def process_frame(self, frame_bgr, camera="left", world_pos=None):
        """Run one frame from one camera. Returns list of per-detection result dicts."""
        import cv2
        H, W = frame_bgr.shape[:2]
        rgb = cv2.cvtColor(frame_bgr, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
        res = self.detector.predict(frame_bgr, conf=self.rt["detector_conf"],
                                    imgsz=self.rt["detector_imgsz"], verbose=False)[0]
        boxes = [tuple(map(float, b)) for b in res.boxes.xyxy.cpu().numpy()] if res.boxes else []

        tracker = self._tracker(camera)
        matched = tracker.update(boxes, self.frame_idx)
        out = []
        for track, box in matched:
            cls, conf, margin = None, 0.0, 0.0
            if self._classifiable(box, W, H):
                x0, y0, x1, y1 = (int(max(0, box[0])), int(max(0, box[1])),
                                  int(min(W, box[2])), int(min(H, box[3])))
                crop = rgb[y0:y1, x0:x1]
                if crop.size:
                    cls, conf, margin = self.clf.predict(crop)
                    track.add_obs(cls, conf, margin, self.frame_idx)
            state, info = self.policy.evaluate(track)
            info["camera"] = camera
            out.append({"bbox": box, "state": state, "cls": cls, "conf": conf,
                        "margin": margin, "camera": camera, "track": track.id,
                        "request_reobserve": info["request_reobserve"], "info": info})
        self.frame_idx += 1
        return out

"""Build Set 1 TensorRT engines — RUN THIS ON THE JETSON ORIN NANO.

TensorRT engines are tied to the device's GPU arch + TRT version, so build on-device:
    python deployment/build_set1_tensorrt.py --half

Detector: built from best.pt via Ultralytics (handles YOLO graph + NMS).
Classifier: built from best.onnx via trtexec (must be on PATH on JetPack).
FP16 (--half) is the recommended speed/accuracy trade-off on Orin Nano.
"""

import argparse
import os
import shutil
import subprocess

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))


def build_detector(half):
    from ultralytics import YOLO
    p = os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set1", "detector", "best.pt")
    path = YOLO(p).export(format="engine", imgsz=640, half=half, dynamic=False)
    print("detector engine ->", path)


def build_classifier(half):
    cdir = os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set1", "classifier")
    onnx = os.path.join(cdir, "best.onnx")
    if not os.path.isfile(onnx):
        raise SystemExit("Run export_set1_onnx.py first to produce classifier best.onnx")
    engine = os.path.join(cdir, "best.engine")
    trtexec = shutil.which("trtexec") or "/usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec"
    cmd = [trtexec, f"--onnx={onnx}", f"--saveEngine={engine}"]
    if half:
        cmd.append("--fp16")
    print("running:", " ".join(cmd))
    subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
    print("classifier engine ->", engine)

1	"""Lightweight multi-object tracker (IoU association) for multi-frame voting.
2	
3	Keeps a short classification history per tracked object so the decision policy can
4	require several consistent observations before committing to a pickup. Set-agnostic.
5	"""
6	
7	from collections import deque
8	
9	
10	def iou(a, b):
11	    ax0, ay0, ax1, ay1 = a
12	    bx0, by0, bx1, by1 = b
13	    ix0, iy0 = max(ax0, bx0), max(ay0, by0)
14	    ix1, iy1 = min(ax1, bx1), min(ay1, by1)
15	    iw, ih = max(0.0, ix1 - ix0), max(0.0, iy1 - iy0)
16	    inter = iw * ih
17	    ua = (ax1 - ax0) * (ay1 - ay0) + (bx1 - bx0) * (by1 - by0) - inter
18	    return inter / ua if ua > 0 else 0.0
19	
20	
21	class Track:
22	    _next_id = 0
23	
24	    def __init__(self, bbox, frame_idx, window):
25	        self.id = Track._next_id

"""Lightweight multi-object tracker (IoU association) for multi-frame voting.

Keeps a short classification history per tracked object so the decision policy can
require several consistent observations before committing to a pickup. Set-agnostic.
"""

from collections import deque


def iou(a, b):
    ax0, ay0, ax1, ay1 = a
    bx0, by0, bx1, by1 = b
    ix0, iy0 = max(ax0, bx0), max(ay0, by0)
    ix1, iy1 = min(ax1, bx1), min(ay1, by1)
    iw, ih = max(0.0, ix1 - ix0), max(0.0, iy1 - iy0)
    inter = iw * ih
    ua = (ax1 - ax0) * (ay1 - ay0) + (bx1 - bx0) * (by1 - by0) - inter
    return inter / ua if ua > 0 else 0.0


class Track:
    _next_id = 0

    def __init__(self, bbox, frame_idx, window):
        self.id = Track._next_id
        Track._next_id += 1
        self.bbox = bbox
        self.last_seen = frame_idx
        self.hits = 1
        self.history = deque(maxlen=window)   # list of dicts: {cls, conf, margin, frame}

    def update(self, bbox, frame_idx):
        self.bbox = bbox
        self.last_seen = frame_idx
        self.hits += 1

    def add_obs(self, cls, conf, margin, frame_idx):
        self.history.append({"cls": cls, "conf": conf, "margin": margin, "frame": frame_idx})


class Tracker:
    def __init__(self, iou_thr=0.3, max_age=10, window=7):
        self.iou_thr = iou_thr
        self.max_age = max_age
        self.window = window
        self.tracks = []

    def update(self, detections, frame_idx):
        """detections: list of bbox (x0,y0,x1,y1). Returns matched [(track, bbox), ...]."""
        unmatched = list(range(len(detections)))
        matched = []
        # Greedy IoU association, best pairs first.
        pairs = []
        for ti, tr in enumerate(self.tracks):
            for di in unmatched:
                v = iou(tr.bbox, detections[di])
                if v >= self.iou_thr:
                    pairs.append((v, ti, di))
        pairs.sort(reverse=True)
        used_t, used_d = set(), set()
        for _, ti, di in pairs:
            if ti in used_t or di in used_d:
                continue
            used_t.add(ti); used_d.add(di)
            self.tracks[ti].update(detections[di], frame_idx)
            matched.append((self.tracks[ti], detections[di]))
        for di in unmatched:
            if di not in used_d:
                tr = Track(detections[di], frame_idx, self.window)
                self.tracks.append(tr)
                matched.append((tr, detections[di]))
        # Age out stale tracks.
        self.tracks = [t for t in self.tracks if frame_idx - t.last_seen <= self.max_age]
        return matched

aiTitle: Jetson에서 USB 웹캠 모델 테스트 설정

content.text:
확인 끝. 코드 근거를 다 봤고, 실제로 문제가 될 지점들을 짚었어요.

먼저 결론부터: **같은 Nuroum V11을 쓰니 카메라 "종류"에 대한 가정(1280×720, 90° HFOV, fx=fy=640)은 유지되지만**, 실제 테스트에서 그 가정이 코드상 자동으로 보장되지 않고, "2대"로 늘리는 것 자체가 흔드는 부분이 몇 군데 있습니다. 아래에 나눠서 정리할게요.

---

## A. Jetson에서 해야 하는 것들

### 1) 런타임 스택 설치 (JetPack, aarch64)
- JetPack 버전 확인 후 그에 맞는 **PyTorch/torchvision (NVIDIA Jetson 빌드)**, **onnxruntime-gpu(TensorRT EP 포함)**, **ultralytics**, **OpenCV(V4L2 지원)** 설치.
- 분류기는 [set1_pipeline.py:40-53](runtime/set1_pipeline.py#L40-L53)에서 `best.onnx`가 있으면 `onnxruntime` + `TensorrtExecutionProvider`를 쓰고, 없으면 torch로 폴백합니다. 즉 onnxruntime-gpu가 없으면 CPU로 떨어질 수 있으니 반드시 GPU 빌드로.

### 2) 모델을 Jetson에서 TensorRT로 빌드
엔진은 디바이스 GPU arch + TRT 버전에 묶이므로 **반드시 Jetson 위에서** 빌드:
```
python deployment/export_set2_onnx.py      # 분류기 onnx 생성
python deployment/build_set2_tensorrt.py --half
```
([build_set1_tensorrt.py](deployment/build_set1_tensorrt.py) 헤더에도 "RUN THIS ON THE JETSON"이라고 적혀 있음.)

⚠️ **주의 (코드 갭 1):** Set2 검출기는 [set2_pipeline.py:91-96](runtime/set2_pipeline.py#L91-L96)에서 `engine → onnx → pt` 순으로 자동 선택하지만, **Set1 검출기는 [set1_pipeline.py:80](runtime/set1_pipeline.py#L80)에서 `best.pt`로 하드코딩**되어 있어요. Set1은 엔진을 빌드해도 자동으로 쓰지 않고 `.pt`(torch+ultralytics 경로)로 돕니다. Set1을 제대로 가속하려면 이 줄을 Set2처럼 폴백 체인으로 고쳐야 합니다. (지금 테스트는 Set2/Nuroum 위주로 보이니 당장은 Set2로 검증하고, Set1은 이 수정만 해두면 됨.)

### 3) 카메라 열기 — **해상도/코덱을 명시적으로 설정** (가장 중요)
현재 [run_perception.py:43,86-91](deployment/run_perception.py#L43)은 전부 `cv2.VideoCapture(src)`만 하고 **해상도도 FOURCC도 설정하지 않습니다.** 즉 웹캠의 기본 모드(대개 640×480 또는 MJPG/YUYV 협상 결과)로 잡힙니다. 모델 가정은 1280×720이므로 이걸 강제해야 함:
```python
cap.set(cv2.CAP_PROP_FOURCC, cv2.VideoWriter_fourcc(*"MJPG"))
cap.set(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_WIDTH, 1280)
cap.set(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_HEIGHT, 720)
cap.set(cv2.CAP_PROP_BUFFERSIZE, 1)   # 지연 누적 방지
```
설정 후 `cap.get(...)`으로 실제 적용값을 반드시 확인(웹캠이 요청을 무시하고 다른 모드로 줄 수 있음).

### 4) 카메라 식별 안정화
`/dev/video0`, `/dev/video1` 인덱스는 재부팅/재연결 시 바뀝니다. Set2는 `--left`(yaw +30°) / `--right`(yaw −30°) 매핑이 의미가 있으니, `/dev/v4l/by-id/...` 심볼릭 링크로 좌우를 고정해서 넘기세요. (좌우가 바뀌면 per-camera 검출 자체는 돌지만, 재관측 방향 지시·항법 융합 좌표가 뒤집힘.)

### 5) 사전 점검 순서
1. `v4l2-ctl --list-devices`, `--list-formats-ext`로 각 카메라가 실제 720p/MJPG를 지원하는지, 몇 fps인지 확인.
2. 카메라 1대로 `--set set2 --target <fruit> --source /dev/... --show` 실행 → 검출/분류/PICKUP 상태 뜨는지, fps 확인.
3. 그 다음 2대(`--left/--right`)로 확장.

---

## B. 카메라 추가·실환경 때문에 흔들리는 모델 가정

### 1) 해상도·화각 → **픽셀 기반 게이트가 전부 여기에 묶여 있음** (최대 리스크)
[configs/set1.yaml](configs/set1.yaml#L131) runtime의 `min_bbox_px=40`, `pickup_min_bbox_px=90`, `reject_truncation_px=3` 등은 **절대 픽셀값**이고, [set1_pipeline.py:86-95](runtime/set1_pipeline.py#L86-L95)에서 그대로 적용됩니다. 이 값들은 **1280×720 + HFOV 90°에서 true-size 물체를 렌더한** 시뮬 기준으로 잡힌 값이에요.

- 실제 캡처가 640×480으로 잡히면 물체 픽셀 크기가 ~절반 → `pickup_min_bbox_px=90`에 도달하려면 로봇이 훨씬 더 가까이 가야 하고, 사실상 PICKUP_READY가 잘 안 뜹니다. (반대로 `min_bbox_area_ratio=0.004`는 비율이라 해상도에 강건 → 두 게이트가 서로 어긋남.) → **A-3에서 720p를 강제하면 해결.**
- 더 근본적인 건 **실제 Nuroum V11의 720p 실측 HFOV가 정말 90°인가**입니다. 웹캠은 해상도/모드에 따라 센서 크롭이 달라 화각이 바뀌는 경우가 흔합니다. 실측 FOV ≠ 90°면, (a) 같은 거리에서 물체의 겉보기 크기가 학습 분포와 어긋나고, (b) 거리 게이트(`pickup_min_bbox_px`)가 미세하게 오정렬됩니다. → 아레나에 물체 하나 놓고 알려진 거리에서 bbox 픽셀폭을 재서 시뮬 값과 비교해 보정하는 걸 권장.

### 2) "2대"로 인한 USB 대역폭 → 간접적으로 해상도 가정을 깸
2× 720p를 한 버스에서 돌리면 **비압축(YUYV)은 대역폭 초과**로 한쪽이 안 열리거나 fps/해상도가 강제로 떨어집니다. 코드가 FOURCC를 안 잡으니 OpenCV가 YUYV로 협상하면 바로 이 문제. → **MJPG 강제 + 가능하면 서로 다른 USB 컨트롤러/파워드 허브**에 분산. (즉 카메라 추가로 성능이 흔들린다면 대부분 여기서 시작됨.)

### 3) **공유 `frame_idx` 때문에 트래커 수명이 절반으로 (코드 갭 2)**
[set2_pipeline.py:129-155](runtime/set2_pipeline.py#L129-L155)에서 `self.frame_idx`는 카메라별 트래커가 **공유**하고 `process_frame` 호출마다(즉 left·right 각각) 증가합니다. 그런데 [tracking.py:73](runtime/tracking.py#L73)의 나이 판정은 `frame_idx - last_seen <= max_age`(프레임 단위).
- left/right를 번갈아 호출하면 left 트랙은 left 프레임에서만 갱신되는데 `frame_idx`는 양쪽에서 다 올라가므로, **`track_max_age=10`이 사실상 left 5프레임 수명**이 됩니다. `vote_window=7`, `min_confirmations=3`의 "프레임" 의미도 카메라 수에 따라 달라짐.
- 단일 카메라로 튜닝된 트래킹/투표가 2대에서는 더 빨리 트랙을 잃고, 확정에 필요한 유효 관측 프레임이 줄어듭니다. → 카메라별 프레임 카운터를 분리하거나, `track_max_age`를 카메라 수만큼 키우는 보정 필요.

### 4) fps 하락의 부작용
2 스트림 = 2배 추론 부하 → fps 하락. 위 수명/투표가 전부 프레임 단위라, 벽시계 기준으로는 트랙 유지·확정 타이밍이 달라집니다. TensorRT FP16 엔진으로 처리량을 확보하는 게 이 영향을 줄이는 핵심.

### 5) 실환경 컬러/노출 (sim-to-real, 카메라 무관하지만 같이 터짐)
웹캠 auto-exposure/AWB는 시뮬 도메인 랜덤화와 다릅니다. Set1 흰색 물체는 그나마 조명 DR로 어느 정도 견디지만, **Set2 과일 텍스처는 메모리상 아직 placeholder**([set2-fruit-cube-pipeline] 기준)이라 실제 과일 사진으로 재학습 전까지 분류 정확도는 신뢰하지 마세요. 테스트 시 노출/화이트밸런스를 수동 고정하면 프레임 간 변동을 줄일 수 있습니다.

---

## 요약: 지금 당장 할 것
1. `run_perception.py`에 **MJPG + 1280×720 + BUFFERSIZE=1** 명시 (안 하면 A·B의 절반이 무너짐).
2. Jetson에서 **TensorRT 엔진 빌드**, Set1 검출기 `.pt` 하드코딩([set1_pipeline.py:80](runtime/set1_pipeline.py#L80))을 Set2식 폴백으로 수정.
3. `/dev/v4l/by-id`로 좌우 고정, USB 버스 분산.
4. Nuroum V11 **720p 실측 HFOV 확인** + 알려진 거리에서 bbox 픽셀 검증.
5. 2대 사용 시 **공유 `frame_idx` → 트랙 수명 절반** 이슈 인지하고 `track_max_age`/투표 파라미터 보정.

1번(캡처 설정)이 실제 테스트의 성패를 가장 크게 좌우합니다. 원하시면 `run_perception.py`의 캡처 설정과 Set1 검출기 폴백 수정을 바로 패치해드릴까요?
A. Jetson에서 해야 하는 것들 우선 이것부터 더 명세히 알려줘
더 정밀하게 명세하려면 현재 저장소 상태(모델 파일 존재 여부, README에 이미 적힌 절차, export 스크립트 전체)를 확인해야 정확합니다. 잠깐 볼게요.

=== README head ===
# Polyhedra detection (cube · octahedron · dodecahedron · icosahedron)

A lightweight YOLO11n detector for 4 types of 3D-printed solids, trained entirely on
synthetic images generated in **Isaac Sim Replicator**, and exported to **ONNX +
TensorRT** for the **Jetson Orin Nano**.

| STL (`datasets/`) | class id | name | faces |
|---|---|---|---|
| `6C1.STL`        | 0 | cube         | 6  |
| `8C1.STL`        | 1 | octahedron   | 8  |
| `12C1_Fixed.STL` | 2 | dodecahedron | 12 |
| `20C1.STL`       | 3 | icosahedron  | 20 |

## Layout

```
configs/
  classes.py            # single source of truth for the class<->id mapping
  polyhedra.yaml        # YOLO dataset config (4 classes)
isaac/                  # runs in Isaac Sim's python (NOT the yolo venv)
  convert_stl_to_usd.py # STL -> USD, mm->m, re-centered   (run once)
  generate_replicator.py# domain-randomized scenes -> YOLO dataset
  assets/usd/           # produced USD meshes
scripts/                # runs in the local yolo/ venv
  train.py              # YOLO11n training
  detect.py             # inference (.pt / .onnx / .engine, image / video / camera)
  export_jetson.py      # ONNX + TensorRT export
datasets/polyhedra/     # generated images/ + labels/  (train/ val/)
```

## Pipeline

### 1. Generate synthetic data — on a machine with Isaac Sim

Isaac Sim is **not** installed in this repo's venv; install it via the Omniverse
launcher or `pip install isaacsim` (4.x). Then:

```bash
# from a machine with Isaac Sim, in this repo root
python isaac/convert_stl_to_usd.py                       # STL -> isaac/assets/usd/*.usd
python isaac/generate_replicator.py --frames 4000 --val-ratio 0.15
```

This randomizes pose (full SO(3)), scale, object count, camera orbit, lighting,
ground colour and per-object PBR colour, and writes Ultralytics YOLO labels directly
(`class cx cy w h`, normalized). Tight 2D boxes come from the
`bounding_box_2d_tight` annotator; off-frame and <25%-visible objects are dropped.
Start at ~4k frames; scale to 10k+ if val mAP plateaus.

> Isaac Sim's Replicator API drifts between versions. If `rep.randomizer.color` or a
> light attribute name errors on your build, that line is the thing to adjust — the
> writer and scene structure are version-stable.

### 2. Train — local `yolo/` venv (RTX 4070 Ti Super)

```bash
yolo/bin/python scripts/train.py --epochs 100 --batch 32
# -> runs/detect/polyhedra/weights/best.pt
```

=== README head ===
# Polyhedra detection (cube · octahedron · dodecahedron · icosahedron)

A lightweight YOLO11n detector for 4 types of 3D-printed solids, trained entirely on
synthetic images generated in **Isaac Sim Replicator**, and exported to **ONNX +
TensorRT** for the **Jetson Orin Nano**.

| STL (`datasets/`) | class id | name | faces |
|---|---|---|---|
| `6C1.STL`        | 0 | cube         | 6  |
| `8C1.STL`        | 1 | octahedron   | 8  |
| `12C1_Fixed.STL` | 2 | dodecahedron | 12 |
| `20C1.STL`       | 3 | icosahedron  | 20 |

## Layout

```
configs/
  classes.py            # single source of truth for the class<->id mapping
  polyhedra.yaml        # YOLO dataset config (4 classes)
isaac/                  # runs in Isaac Sim's python (NOT the yolo venv)
  convert_stl_to_usd.py # STL -> USD, mm->m, re-centered   (run once)
  generate_replicator.py# domain-randomized scenes -> YOLO dataset
  assets/usd/           # produced USD meshes
scripts/                # runs in the local yolo/ venv
  train.py              # YOLO11n training
  detect.py             # inference (.pt / .onnx / .engine, image / video / camera)
  export_jetson.py      # ONNX + TensorRT export
datasets/polyhedra/     # generated images/ + labels/  (train/ val/)
```

## Pipeline

### 1. Generate synthetic data — on a machine with Isaac Sim

Isaac Sim is **not** installed in this repo's venv; install it via the Omniverse
launcher or `pip install isaacsim` (4.x). Then:

```bash
# from a machine with Isaac Sim, in this repo root
python isaac/convert_stl_to_usd.py                       # STL -> isaac/assets/usd/*.usd
python isaac/generate_replicator.py --frames 4000 --val-ratio 0.15
```

This randomizes pose (full SO(3)), scale, object count, camera orbit, lighting,
ground colour and per-object PBR colour, and writes Ultralytics YOLO labels directly
(`class cx cy w h`, normalized). Tight 2D boxes come from the
`bounding_box_2d_tight` annotator; off-frame and <25%-visible objects are dropped.
Start at ~4k frames; scale to 10k+ if val mAP plateaus.

> Isaac Sim's Replicator API drifts between versions. If `rep.randomizer.color` or a
> light attribute name errors on your build, that line is the thing to adjust — the
> writer and scene structure are version-stable.

### 2. Train — local `yolo/` venv (RTX 4070 Ti Super)

```bash
yolo/bin/python scripts/train.py --epochs 100 --batch 32
# -> runs/detect/polyhedra/weights/best.pt
```

"""Export Set 2 models to ONNX (portable; build TensorRT on the Jetson itself).

    yolo/bin/python deployment/export_set2_onnx.py
Outputs:
    models/set2/detector/best.onnx
    models/set2/classifier/best.onnx
"""

import json
import os

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))


def export_detector():
    from ultralytics import YOLO
    p = os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set2", "detector", "best.pt")
    path = YOLO(p).export(format="onnx", imgsz=640, opset=12, simplify=True, dynamic=False)
    print("detector ONNX ->", path)


def export_classifier():
    import torch
    from runtime.set2_pipeline import build_classifier_torch
    cdir = os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set2", "classifier")
    classes = json.load(open(os.path.join(cdir, "classes.json")))
    imgsz = json.load(open(os.path.join(cdir, "temperature.json")))["imgsz"]
    model = build_classifier_torch(len(classes))
    model.load_state_dict(torch.load(os.path.join(cdir, "best.pt"), map_location="cpu"))
    model.eval()
    dummy = torch.zeros(1, 3, imgsz, imgsz)
    out = os.path.join(cdir, "best.onnx")
    torch.onnx.export(model, dummy, out, input_names=["input"], output_names=["logits"],
                      opset_version=12, dynamic_axes=None)
    print("classifier ONNX ->", out)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys
    sys.path.insert(0, ROOT)
    export_detector()
    export_classifier()

"""Build Set 2 TensorRT engines — RUN THIS ON THE JETSON ORIN NANO.

TensorRT engines are tied to the device's GPU arch + TRT version, so build on-device:
    python deployment/build_set2_tensorrt.py --half

Detector: built from best.pt via Ultralytics (handles YOLO graph + NMS).
Classifier: built from best.onnx via trtexec (must be on PATH on JetPack).
FP16 (--half) is the recommended speed/accuracy trade-off on Orin Nano.
"""

import argparse
import os
import shutil
import subprocess

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))


def build_detector(half):
    from ultralytics import YOLO
    p = os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set2", "detector", "best.pt")
    path = YOLO(p).export(format="engine", imgsz=640, half=half, dynamic=False)
    print("detector engine ->", path)


def build_classifier(half):
    cdir = os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set2", "classifier")
    onnx = os.path.join(cdir, "best.onnx")
    if not os.path.isfile(onnx):
        raise SystemExit("Run export_set2_onnx.py first to produce classifier best.onnx")
    engine = os.path.join(cdir, "best.engine")
    trtexec = shutil.which("trtexec") or "/usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec"
    cmd = [trtexec, f"--onnx={onnx}", f"--saveEngine={engine}"]
    if half:
        cmd.append("--fp16")
    print("running:", " ".join(cmd))
    subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
    print("classifier engine ->", engine)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--half", action="store_true", help="FP16 (recommended on Orin Nano)")
    args = ap.parse_args()
    build_detector(args.half)
    build_classifier(args.half)

aiTitle: Jetson에서 USB 웹캠 모델 테스트 설정

content.text:
확인 끝났어요. 중요한 사실 두 가지:
- 가중치는 `.pt`/`.onnx` 모두 디스크에 있지만 [.gitignore](.gitignore)로 **git 추적 제외** → Jetson에 clone하면 가중치가 안 따라옵니다. 별도 전송 필요. `.engine`은 아직 없음(디바이스에서 빌드).
- set2 분류기는 `best.onnx` + **`best.onnx.data`(외부 가중치)** 세트라 둘 다 같이 옮겨야 함.

아래가 Jetson에서 해야 할 것들의 상세 명세입니다. 순서대로 하면 됩니다.

---

## 0. 사전 준비 / 전제 확인

```bash
cat /etc/nv_tegra_release            # L4T/JetPack 버전 확인 (torch·ort·TRT 버전 매칭 기준)
sudo nvpmodel -q                     # 현재 파워모드
sudo nvpmodel -m 0 && sudo jetson_clocks   # 최대 성능(MAXN) + 클럭 고정
```
- JetPack 버전을 먼저 확정해야 아래 2번의 PyTorch/onnxruntime 휠을 맞출 수 있습니다. (Orin Nano는 보통 JetPack 5.x=TRT8/CUDA11.4 또는 6.x=CUDA12.)
- 모니터링 도구: `sudo pip install jetson-stats` → `jtop` (또는 `tegrastats`)로 GPU/전력/온도 확인.

## 1. 저장소 + 가중치 전송
git에는 코드만 있고 가중치는 빠져 있으므로:
```bash
git clone <repo> ~/joon            # 코드
# 가중치는 별도로 (예: scp). set2만 먼저 테스트하면 최소:
#   models/set2/detector/best.pt   (엔진 빌드용)
#   models/set2/classifier/best.onnx  +  best.onnx.data   (런타임용)
#   models/set2/classifier/best.pt (onnx 재생성/폴백용)
#   models/set2/classifier/{classes.json,temperature.json}
```
⚠️ 분류기 `best.onnx`는 **`best.onnx.data`와 같은 폴더에 함께** 있어야 로드됩니다. 하나만 옮기면 실패.

## 2. Jetson용 파이썬 환경
JetPack에 맞는 aarch64 휠로 설치(버전 핀은 JetPack에 맞춰서):
- **PyTorch/torchvision**: NVIDIA Jetson 전용 휠(공식 Jetson PyTorch 인덱스). x86용 pip torch를 그냥 깔면 CUDA가 안 붙습니다.
- **onnxruntime-gpu**: Jetson용 빌드(TensorRT EP 포함). 분류기 런타임 경로가 이걸 씁니다([set2_pipeline.py:41-45](runtime/set2_pipeline.py#L41-L45)).
- **ultralytics**, **numpy**, **pyyaml**.
- **OpenCV**: JetPack에 V4L2/GStreamer 지원 OpenCV가 대개 기본 포함. 없으면 `python3-opencv`.

검증:
```bash
python3 -c "import torch;print(torch.__version__, torch.cuda.is_available())"
python3 -c "import onnxruntime as ort;print(ort.get_available_providers())"
#  -> TensorrtExecutionProvider / CUDAExecutionProvider 가 보여야 함
python3 -c "import cv2;print(cv2.getBuildInformation())" | grep -iE "V4L|GStreamer"
```

## 3. TensorRT 엔진 빌드 (반드시 디바이스에서)
엔진은 GPU arch + TRT 버전에 묶이므로 Jetson 위에서:
```bash
cd ~/joon
python deployment/export_set2_onnx.py        # onnx 없거나 재생성 필요할 때만
python deployment/build_set2_tensorrt.py --half   # 검출기 best.engine (FP16)
which trtexec || ls /usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec  # 없으면 위 스크립트가 폴백 경로 사용
```
- 검출기: [set2_pipeline.py:91-96](runtime/set2_pipeline.py#L91-L96)가 `engine→onnx→pt` 순으로 잡아서 **`best.engine`이 있으면 자동 사용**.
- 분류기: 참고로 [build_set2_tensorrt.py:26-38](deployment/build_set2_tensorrt.py#L26-L38)이 trtexec로 `classifier/best.engine`을 만들지만, **런타임은 분류기 엔진을 직접 안 씁니다**([FruitClassifier]는 `.onnx`/`.pt`만 확인). 분류기 가속은 onnxruntime의 **TensorRT EP**가 담당(첫 실행 시 EP가 내부 엔진을 빌드/캐시하므로 첫 프레임이 느림). 즉 분류기는 `best.onnx`만 제대로 있으면 됨.
- 첫 실행이 오래 걸리는 걸 피하려면 ORT TRT EP 엔진 캐시를 켜두는 것도 방법(환경변수). 안 켜면 매 실행 첫 프레임에 재빌드.

## 4. 실행 전 필요한 코드 수정 (Jetson 테스트용 필수)
지금 코드 그대로 돌리면 모델 가정이 안 지켜집니다. 최소 2개:

1. **캡처 해상도/코덱 강제** — [run_perception.py:43,86-91](deployment/run_perception.py#L43)의 `cv2.VideoCapture(src)` 직후에:
```python
cap.set(cv2.CAP_PROP_FOURCC, cv2.VideoWriter_fourcc(*"MJPG"))
cap.set(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_WIDTH, 1280)
cap.set(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_HEIGHT, 720)
cap.set(cv2.CAP_PROP_BUFFERSIZE, 1)
```
설정 후 `cap.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_WIDTH/HEIGHT)`로 **실제 적용값 확인**(웹캠이 무시할 수 있음). 이 단계가 픽셀 기반 게이트(min_bbox_px·pickup_min_bbox_px)를 유효하게 만듭니다.

2. **(Set1을 테스트할 경우만)** [set1_pipeline.py:80](runtime/set1_pipeline.py#L80)의 하드코딩된 `best.pt`를 Set2식 `engine→onnx→pt` 폴백으로 교체. 안 하면 Set1은 엔진을 안 쓰고 torch로만 돕니다. (Set2만 볼 거면 지금은 스킵 가능.)

## 5. 카메라 브링업 & 검증
```bash
ls /dev/video*                       # 장치 나열
v4l2-ctl --list-devices              # 어떤 게 Nuroum인지
v4l2-ctl -d /dev/video0 --list-formats-ext   # MJPG로 1280x720@30 지원 확인
ls -l /dev/v4l/by-id/                # 재부팅에도 안 바뀌는 안정 경로
```
- **좌우 고정**: `/dev/v4l/by-id/...` 경로로 `--left`/`--right`를 넘겨 인덱스 뒤바뀜 방지.
- **권한**: 접근 거부 시 `sudo usermod -aG video $USER` 후 재로그인.
- **대역폭(2대 핵심)**: 2×720p는 MJPG여야 안전. 두 카메라를 **서로 다른 USB 컨트롤러**(또는 파워드 허브)에 꽂기. `dmesg | grep -i usb`로 대역폭/재연결 에러 확인.

## 6. 실행 & 관찰
```bash
# 1대 먼저
python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana \
    --source /dev/v4l/by-id/<nuroum-1> --show
# 정상 확인되면 2대
python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana \
    --left /dev/v4l/by-id/<nuroum-1> --right /dev/v4l/by-id/<nuroum-2> --show
```
확인 포인트:
- 콘솔에 검출기 백엔드 출력(`[set2] detector backend: best.engine`)이 엔진인지.
- fps (필요하면 코드에 타이머 추가). jtop으로 GPU 사용률/전력.
- 실제 프레임 해상도가 1280×720으로 잡혔는지.
- `--log`로 애매한 크롭 저장([run_perception.py:115-118](deployment/run_perception.py#L115-L118)) → 나중에 오분류 분석.

## 7. 성능 튜닝 순서
1. `nvpmodel -m 0` + `jetson_clocks` (0번에서 이미 함).
2. 검출기 FP16 엔진 확인, 분류기 ORT TRT EP 확인.
3. 그래도 2대 fps가 부족하면: 검출기 `detector_imgsz`를 640→512로 낮춰보기([configs/set2.yaml] runtime), 또는 두 카메라를 매 프레임 번갈아 처리하지 말고 프레임 스킵.

---

정리하면 **의존성(2)→엔진 빌드(3)→캡처/코드 수정(4)→카메라 브링업(5)→실행(6)** 순서이고, 실제로 사람들이 많이 막히는 곳은 (2) JetPack에 맞는 torch/onnxruntime 휠, (4) 캡처 해상도 강제, (5) 2대 USB 대역폭입니다.

원하시면 4번의 두 코드 수정(캡처 설정 + Set1 폴백)을 지금 바로 패치해드릴까요? Set2만 테스트할 계획이면 캡처 설정만 해도 됩니다.

Set1을 먼저 한다면 Set2와 달라지는 지점이 몇 개 있고, 특히 **"2대 카메라"와 Set1 코드가 안 맞는 부분**이 있어서 그것부터 짚을게요.

## Set1에서 달라지는 점

**전송할 가중치 (Set1용):**
- `models/set1/detector/best.pt` — 엔진 빌드용
- `models/set1/classifier/best.onnx` + `classes.json` + `temperature.json` — 런타임용
- `models/set1/classifier/best.pt` — onnx 재생성/폴백용

참고로 Set1 분류기 onnx는 **단일 파일**이라 Set2와 달리 `best.onnx.data`는 없습니다(전송 편함).

**엔진 빌드 (디바이스에서):**
```bash
python deployment/export_set1_onnx.py       # onnx 재생성 필요시만
```

1. 캡처 해상도/코덱 강제 — [run_perception.py:43](deployment/run_perception.py#L43)의 `run_set1` 캡처 직후 MJPG+1280×720+BUFFERSIZE=1.

## ⚠️ 핵심 문제: Set1은 지금 "2대 카메라"를 지원 안 함

- [run_perception.py:31-64](deployment/run_perception.py#L31-L64)의 `run_set1`은 **`--source` 단일 카메라만** 읽습니다 (`--left`/`--right`는 Set2 전용).
- 게다가 [set1_pipeline.py:82](runtime/set1_pipeline.py#L82)의 `Set1Pipeline`은 **트래커가 1개(공유)**입니다. Set2는 카메라별 트래커였지만 Set1은 아님 → 2대 프레임을 한 파이프라인에 넣으면 서로 다른 시야의 bbox가 한 IoU 트래커에 섞여 트래킹이 깨집니다.

즉 Set1을 2대로 테스트하려면 방식을 정해야 합니다. 이게 제가 작성할 코드를 바꾸므로 먼저 여쭤볼게요.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Onboard perception entry point. Loads ONLY the selected set's models.

    python deployment/run_perception.py --set set1 --target dodecahedron --source 0
    python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --source 0
    python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --left 0 --right 1

For Set 1: detect white polyhedra from afar, classify shape only when close/reliable,
vote across frames, and announce TARGET_CONFIRMED / PICKUP_READY conservatively.

For Set 2: detect cube candidates from afar, classify the visible fruit only when close
and the fruit is actually visible, never pick an 'unknown' cube, request a viewpoint
change when a cube stays unknown, and confirm the target fruit across frames before pickup.
"""

import argparse
import os
import sys

import yaml

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
sys.path.insert(0, ROOT)

STATE_COLOR = {"SEARCHING": (180, 180, 180), "TARGET_CONFIRMED": (0, 200, 255),
               "PICKUP_READY": (0, 230, 0), "GIVE_UP": (0, 0, 200),
               # Set 2 states:
               "UNKNOWN_CUBE": (160, 160, 160), "NON_TARGET_FRUIT": (0, 120, 220),
               "TARGET_CANDIDATE": (0, 200, 255), "REJECTED": (0, 0, 200)}


def run_set1(cfg, args):
    import cv2
    from runtime.set1_pipeline import Set1Pipeline
    from runtime.logging import FailureLogger

    if args.target not in cfg["classes"]["shapes"]:
        raise SystemExit(f"--target must be one of {cfg['classes']['shapes']}")
    pipe = Set1Pipeline(cfg, args.target)
    logger = FailureLogger(os.path.join(ROOT, "runtime_logs", "set1"), enabled=args.log)
    print(f"[set1] target={args.target}; loaded detector + classifier. Press q to quit.")

    src = int(args.source) if str(args.source).isdigit() else args.source
    cap = cv2.VideoCapture(src)
    while cap.isOpened():
        ok, frame = cap.read()
        if not ok:
            break
        for r in pipe.process_frame(frame):
            x0, y0, x1, y1 = (int(v) for v in r["bbox"])
            color = STATE_COLOR.get(r["state"], (200, 200, 200))
            label = f"{r['cls'] or 'polyhedron'} {r['conf']:.2f} {r['state']}"
            cv2.rectangle(frame, (x0, y0), (x1, y1), color, 2)
            cv2.putText(frame, label, (x0, max(0, y0 - 6)),
                        cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.5, color, 1)
            if r["state"] == "PICKUP_READY":
                print(f"PICKUP_READY: {args.target} @ bbox {r['bbox']}  info={r['info']}")
            if args.log and (r["cls"] == "unknown" or (r["cls"] and r["margin"] < cfg["runtime"]["margin_threshold"])):
                logger.maybe_log(cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB), r, reason=r["cls"] or "lowmargin")
        if args.show:
            cv2.imshow("set1", frame)
            if cv2.waitKey(1) & 0xFF == ord("q"):
                break
    cap.release()
    cv2.destroyAllWindows()


def run_set2(cfg, args):
    """Detect cube candidates, classify visible fruit conservatively, confirm target.

    Reads from one or two cameras (--left/--right, or --source for a single camera).
    A cube 'unknown' in one camera can be identified by the other; a cube that stays
    unknown raises a re-observe request the navigator should honour by moving."""
    import cv2
    from runtime.set2_pipeline import Set2Pipeline
    from runtime.logging import FailureLogger

    fruits = cfg["classes"]["fruits"]
    if args.target not in fruits:
        raise SystemExit(f"--target must be one of {fruits}")
    pipe = Set2Pipeline(cfg, args.target)
    logger = FailureLogger(os.path.join(ROOT, "runtime_logs", "set2"), enabled=args.log)

    # Camera sources: prefer explicit --left/--right; else a single --source as 'left'.
    cams = {}
    if args.left is not None:
        cams["left"] = cv2.VideoCapture(int(args.left) if str(args.left).isdigit() else args.left)
    if args.right is not None:
        cams["right"] = cv2.VideoCapture(int(args.right) if str(args.right).isdigit() else args.right)
    if not cams:
        src = int(args.source) if str(args.source).isdigit() else args.source
        cams["left"] = cv2.VideoCapture(src)
    print(f"[set2] target={args.target}; cameras={list(cams)}; loaded detector + classifier.")

    while all(c.isOpened() for c in cams.values()):
        any_ok = False
        for cam_name, cap in cams.items():
            ok, frame = cap.read()
            if not ok:
                continue
            any_ok = True
            for r in pipe.process_frame(frame, camera=cam_name):
                x0, y0, x1, y1 = (int(v) for v in r["bbox"])
                color = STATE_COLOR.get(r["state"], (200, 200, 200))
                label = f"{cam_name[:1]} {r['cls'] or 'cube'} {r['conf']:.2f} {r['state']}"
                cv2.rectangle(frame, (x0, y0), (x1, y1), color, 2)
                cv2.putText(frame, label, (x0, max(0, y0 - 6)),
                            cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.5, color, 1)
                if r["state"] == "PICKUP_READY":
                    print(f"PICKUP_READY: {args.target} @ {cam_name} bbox {r['bbox']} info={r['info']}")
                if r["request_reobserve"]:
                    print(f"RE-OBSERVE: track {r['track']} on {cam_name} stays unknown -> "
                          f"move to a new viewpoint. info={r['info']}")
                    # In a full system the navigator moves, then calls:
                    #   pipe.note_reobserved(cam_name, r['track'])
                if args.log and (r["cls"] == "unknown"
                                 or (r["cls"] and r["margin"] < cfg["runtime"]["margin_threshold"])):
                    logger.maybe_log(cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB), r,
                                     reason=r["cls"] or "lowmargin")
            if args.show:
                cv2.imshow(f"set2-{cam_name}", frame)
        if args.show and (cv2.waitKey(1) & 0xFF == ord("q")):
            break
        if not any_ok:
            break
    for c in cams.values():
        c.release()
    cv2.destroyAllWindows()


def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--set", dest="set_name", required=True, choices=["set1", "set2"])
    ap.add_argument("--config", default=None)
    ap.add_argument("--target", default="dodecahedron",
                    help="announced target: set1 shape or set2 fruit")
    ap.add_argument("--source", default="0", help="camera index or video/image path")
    ap.add_argument("--left", default=None, help="set2: left camera index/path")
    ap.add_argument("--right", default=None, help="set2: right camera index/path")
    ap.add_argument("--show", action="store_true")
    ap.add_argument("--log", action="store_true", help="save uncertain crops for the data loop")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    cfg_path = args.config or os.path.join(ROOT, "configs", f"{args.set_name}.yaml")
    cfg = yaml.safe_load(open(cfg_path, encoding="utf-8"))

    if args.set_name == "set1":
        run_set1(cfg, args)
    else:
        run_set2(cfg, args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""Set 1 runtime perception: detector -> crop -> conservative classifier -> track -> vote.

Loads only the Set 1 models (set1_polyhedron_detector + set1_shape_classifier). The
detector (ultralytics, .pt/.onnx/.engine) finds polyhedra at high recall; each crop is
classified by MobileNetV3 (torch .pt or .onnx) with temperature-calibrated confidence;
results feed the tracker + DecisionPolicy.
"""

import json
import os

import numpy as np

from runtime.tracking import Tracker
from runtime.decision_policy import DecisionPolicy

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))


def build_classifier_torch(num_classes):
    import torch.nn as nn
    from torchvision import models
    m = models.mobilenet_v3_small(weights=None)
    m.classifier[3] = nn.Linear(m.classifier[3].in_features, num_classes)
    return m


class ShapeClassifier:
    """Loads a MobileNetV3 classifier (.pt or .onnx) + calibration metadata."""

    def __init__(self, model_dir):
        self.classes = json.load(open(os.path.join(model_dir, "classes.json")))
        meta = json.load(open(os.path.join(model_dir, "temperature.json")))
        self.T = meta["temperature"]
        self.imgsz = meta["imgsz"]
        self.mean = np.array(meta["mean"], np.float32)
        self.std = np.array(meta["std"], np.float32)

        pt, onnx = os.path.join(model_dir, "best.pt"), os.path.join(model_dir, "best.onnx")
        if os.path.isfile(onnx):
            import onnxruntime as ort
            self.backend = "onnx"
            self.sess = ort.InferenceSession(onnx, providers=[
                "TensorrtExecutionProvider", "CUDAExecutionProvider", "CPUExecutionProvider"])
            self.inp = self.sess.get_inputs()[0].name
        else:
            import torch
            self.backend = "torch"
            self.torch = torch
            self.device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
            self.model = build_classifier_torch(len(self.classes)).to(self.device).eval()
            self.model.load_state_dict(torch.load(pt, map_location=self.device))

    def _pre(self, crop_rgb):
        import cv2
        img = cv2.resize(crop_rgb, (self.imgsz, self.imgsz)).astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        img = (img - self.mean) / self.std
        return np.transpose(img, (2, 0, 1))[None].astype(np.float32)

    def predict(self, crop_rgb):
        x = self._pre(crop_rgb)
        if self.backend == "onnx":
            logits = self.sess.run(None, {self.inp: x})[0][0]
        else:
            with self.torch.no_grad():
                logits = self.model(self.torch.from_numpy(x).to(self.device)).cpu().numpy()[0]
        z = logits / self.T
        e = np.exp(z - z.max()); p = e / e.sum()
        order = p.argsort()[::-1]
        top1, top2 = order[0], order[1]
        return self.classes[top1], float(p[top1]), float(p[top1] - p[top2])


class Set1Pipeline:
    def __init__(self, cfg, target_shape):
        from ultralytics import YOLO
        rt = cfg["runtime"]
        self.rt = rt
        self.target = target_shape
        self.detector = YOLO(os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set1", "detector", "best.pt"))
        self.clf = ShapeClassifier(os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set1", "classifier"))
        self.tracker = Tracker(rt["track_iou"], rt["track_max_age"], rt["vote_window"])
        self.policy = DecisionPolicy(rt, target_shape)
        self.frame_idx = 0

    def _classifiable(self, box, W, H):
        x0, y0, x1, y1 = box
        if min(x1 - x0, y1 - y0) < self.rt["min_bbox_px"]:
            return False
        if (x1 - x0) * (y1 - y0) / (W * H) < self.rt["min_bbox_area_ratio"]:
            return False
        m = self.rt["reject_truncation_px"]
        if x0 <= m or y0 <= m or x1 >= W - m or y1 >= H - m:
            return False
        return True

    def process_frame(self, frame_bgr):
        """Run one frame. Returns list of {bbox, state, cls, conf, info}."""
        import cv2
        H, W = frame_bgr.shape[:2]
        rgb = cv2.cvtColor(frame_bgr, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
        res = self.detector.predict(frame_bgr, conf=self.rt["detector_conf"],
                                    imgsz=self.rt["detector_imgsz"], verbose=False)[0]
        boxes = [tuple(map(float, b)) for b in res.boxes.xyxy.cpu().numpy()] if res.boxes else []

        matched = self.tracker.update(boxes, self.frame_idx)
        out = []
        for track, box in matched:
            cls, conf, margin = None, 0.0, 0.0
            if self._classifiable(box, W, H):
                x0, y0, x1, y1 = (int(max(0, box[0])), int(max(0, box[1])),
                                  int(min(W, box[2])), int(min(H, box[3])))
                crop = rgb[y0:y1, x0:x1]
                if crop.size:
                    cls, conf, margin = self.clf.predict(crop)
                    track.add_obs(cls, conf, margin, self.frame_idx)
            state, info = self.policy.evaluate(track)
            out.append({"bbox": box, "state": state, "cls": cls,
                        "conf": conf, "margin": margin, "info": info})
        self.frame_idx += 1
        return out

두 수정 모두 반영됐고 문법 OK입니다. 이제 **Set1 · 1대 카메라** 테스트 절차 정리:

## Set1 단일 카메라 테스트 순서

**1. 가중치 전송** (git엔 없음)
```
models/set1/detector/best.pt
models/set1/classifier/best.onnx, classes.json, temperature.json
models/set1/classifier/best.pt   (onnx 재생성/폴백용)
```

**2. Jetson 파워/환경**
```bash
sudo nvpmodel -m 0 && sudo jetson_clocks
python3 -c "import onnxruntime as ort; print(ort.get_available_providers())"  # TRT/CUDA EP 확인
```

**3. 엔진 빌드 (디바이스에서)**
```bash
python3 deployment/build_set1_tensorrt.py --half
```

**4. 카메라 확인**
```bash
v4l2-ctl --list-devices
v4l2-ctl -d /dev/video0 --list-formats-ext   # MJPG 1280x720 지원 확인
```

**5. 실행**
```bash
python3 deployment/run_perception.py --set set1 --target dodecahedron \
    --source /dev/v4l/by-id/<nuroum> --show --log
```

**확인 포인트:**
- 콘솔 첫 줄 `[set1] detector backend: best.engine` (엔진 사용 확인 — 안 뜨고 `best.pt`면 3번 실패)
- `[set1] capture 1280x720` (해상도 강제 적용 확인 — 다른 값이면 카메라가 요청 무시한 것)
- 화면에 bbox + 상태(`SEARCHING`/`TARGET_CONFIRMED`/`PICKUP_READY`) 표시
- `--log`로 애매한 크롭이 `runtime_logs/set1/`에 저장됨 → 오분류 분석용

## 남은 유의점 (Set1 특성)
- 시뮬은 흰색 물체 + 조명 랜덤화로 학습 → 실환경에선 **웹캠 auto-exposure/화이트밸런스**가 흰색 물체를 회색/청색조로 밀 수 있음. 가능하면 노출 수동 고정 후 테스트.
- `dodecahedron` vs `icosahedron`이 원래 혼동 지점이니, 이 둘로 테스트하면 분류기 신뢰도(margin)를 잘 보세요.
- 나중에 2대로 갈 때는 앞서 정한 대로 **프로세스 2개** 또는 **dual-cam 지원 추가**를 진행하면 됩니다. (지금 코드는 1대 전용 상태 유지)

2대로 확장할 준비가 되면 말씀해 주세요.

aiTitle: Jetson에서 USB 웹캠 모델 테스트 설정

content.text:
Jetson에 카메라를 꽂은 상태에서 아래로 찾으면 됩니다.

## 1. by-id 경로 나열
```bash
ls -l /dev/v4l/by-id/
```
출력 예시:
```
usb-Nuroum_Nuroum_V11_...-video-index0 -> ../../video0
usb-Nuroum_Nuroum_V11_...-video-index1 -> ../../video1
```
- 파일명에 제조사/모델명(예: `Nuroum_V11`)이 들어갑니다. 그 **전체 이름**이 `<nuroum>` 자리에 들어갈 값이에요:
```bash
--source /dev/v4l/by-id/usb-Nuroum_Nuroum_V11_...-video-index0
```
- 탭 자동완성(`ls /dev/v4l/by-id/` 치고 Tab)으로 긴 이름을 편하게 넣을 수 있습니다.

## 2. ⚠️ index0 을 골라야 함
한 카메라가 보통 `video-index0`, `video-index1` **두 개**로 나옵니다. 실제 영상 캡처가 되는 건 대개 **index0**(index1은 메타데이터 스트림). 어느 게 캡처용인지 확인:
```bash
v4l2-ctl -d /dev/v4l/by-id/usb-Nuroum...-video-index0 --list-formats-ext
```
→ `MJPG`, `1280x720` 등 포맷이 뜨면 그게 캡처용. 아무것도 안 뜨거나 메타데이터만 뜨면 index1을 쓰세요.

## 3. 어느 게 어느 카메라인지 (2대일 때)
by-id 이름만으로 좌/우 구분이 안 되면 하나씩 꽂아보거나, 영상으로 확인:
```bash
v4l2-ctl --list-devices          # 모델명 ↔ /dev/videoN 매핑
# 실제 화면 확인 (X 환경이면)
ffplay /dev/v4l/by-id/usb-Nuroum...-video-index0
```

## 참고
- by-id 경로는 **USB 포트가 같으면 재부팅해도 안 바뀌어서** `--source 0` 같은 숫자 인덱스보다 안정적입니다 (숫자는 부팅/재연결마다 바뀔 수 있음).
- by-id에 모델명이 안 나오고 일반적인 이름(예: `usb-046d_...`)만 나오면, 포트 위치로 고정하는 `ls -l /dev/v4l/by-path/`를 대신 쓰면 됩니다.

1대 테스트니까 index0 하나만 찾아서 `--source`에 넣으면 됩니다.
